title: Inditex data breach: Zara owner Inditex reports major data breach exposing customer transaction records
summary: Zara owner Inditex reported a data breach involving a third party. The company said latest breach exposed transaction-related information but no personal details.

title: Meet Happy Oyster: Alibaba launches new AI video model that turns text prompts into playable 3D worlds
summary: Alibabahas launched the Happy Oyster AI model. The new model is capable of generating interactive 3D environments and videos in real time, allowing users to direct or explore evolving scenes with consistent physics

title: ‘Your job is changing on you’: LinkedIn says AI isn't killing hiring, but will rewrite your skillset by 2030
summary: A LinkedIn executive has revealed that AI isn't behind the hiring slowdown and instead blames it on rising interest rates.

title: Nothing launches the ultimate Android-to-Mac transfer app, quietly takes it down hours later
summary: Nothing has taken down the Warp app from Android and Chrome soon after the launch. The company is yet to share a reason for the removal of the app.

title: YouTube finally lets you turn off Shorts: Check out exact settings to clean up your feed
summary: YouTube now allows users to completely remove Shorts videos from the app, a feature added in 2020. This option is part of the Shorts Feed Limit feature which was first introduced last year.

title: Google brings the Gemini app to Mac, Sundar Pichai says it was built by AI
summary: Google has released the Gemini app for Mac, allowing users to access its AI assistant via a keyboard shortcut and share screens for contextual help. The app was built by Google's own AI coding tool Antigravity, Sundar Pichai revealed

title: White House to give US agencies Anthropic Mythos access: Report
summary: The US government is planning to make a version of ​Anthropic's frontier AI model ​Mythos available to major federal agencies amid concerns ​that the tool could sharply increase cybersecurity risk.

title: OpenAI launches AI model GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
summary: The GPT-Rosalind, named after 20th-century British scientist Rosalind Franklin, is designed to support ‌research ⁠across biochemistry, drug ⁠discovery and translational medicine.

title: Russia-linked Grinex crypto exchange suspends operations after cyber attack
summary: Grinex, a crypto exchange linked to Russia, has halted operations. This follows a cyber attack where assets worth 1 billion roubles were stolen. The exchange claims foreign intelligence services were involved. Grinex was previously sanctioned by the US, UK, and EU. The attack's nature suggests advan

title: Centre forms nodal body to coordinate, develop AI governance norms
summary: The Centre on Thursday announced the formation of the AI Governance and Economic Group (AIGEG) to lead the country's national AI governance strategy. Set to serve as a high-level inter-ministerial body, it will coordinate the development of AI policy across central ministries and institutions.

title: 'Harassment, religious conversion' at Nashik MNC: 2 accused sent in fresh police custody
summary: Two men arrested in a sexual harassment and forced religious conversion case at a Tata Consultancy Services unit in Nashik were sent to police custody. title: Meta to raise Quest VR headset prices in US on rising component costs
summary: Meta Platforms is raising prices for its virtual reality headsets starting April 19 in the United States. This move is driven by increasing costs for memory chips. The entry-level Quest 3S and higher-end Quest 3 models will see price adjustments. Other tech companies are also increasing device price

title: Former Meta contractor Sama to lay off more than 1,000 workers in Kenya
summary: Facebook's parent company Meta has ended its contract with Kenyan firm Sama. This decision will lead to over 1,000 workers being laid off from Sama's Nairobi office. The company provided content moderation services for Meta. A lawsuit filed by former moderators over poor working conditions and menta

title: Gucci-owner Kering aims to launch luxury Google glasses next year: CEO
summary: Gucci is set to launch smart glasses with Google next year. This move marks a significant entry into AI-powered eyewear. The brand aims to revive its iconic classics and adapt to a changing global market. This strategy is part of a larger plan to boost Kering's eyewear and jewelry divisions.

title: Indian companies are moving beyond AI pilots to real world applications: Anthropic’s Irina Ghose
summary: Indian organisations, from large public sector players to SMEs, are engaging more actively with artificial intelligence. They are moving beyond proof-of-concept stages to scaled deployments, said Irina Ghose, managing director, India at Anthropic, on Thursday.

title: Pocket FM hits $450 million ARR in April after accelerating AI-led content creation
summary: Pocket FM hits $200 million ARR after six years of operations in Q4 of FY25, and it achieved about $400 million ARR in Q4 of FY26. Currently, the platform has over 300,000 creators producing more than 80,000 hours of content monthly.
